user_query:
You’re our interim metrics lead for a Series A, sales-led SaaS. Read all the provided data under input/ and deliver a board-ready metrics package. Specifically:

1) Compute our latest month (2026-03) metrics and write them to output/metrics_summary.json with these keys (use numbers, not strings; round to 2 decimals where appropriate):
- period ("2026-03")
- mrr
- arr
- mom_growth (from 2026-02 to 2026-03, decimal not percent)
- cmgr_6m (from 2025-10 end-of-month MRR to 2026-03 end-of-month MRR, 5-month period, decimal not percent)
- ndr (for 2026-03 using starting_mrr, expansion_mrr, contraction_mrr, churned_mrr; decimal not percent)
- gdr (same month; decimal not percent)
- cac (for 2026-03 using that month’s new_customers and S&M spend)
- ltv (ARPU × gross_margin% × (1 / monthly_logo_churn_rate) using 2026-03 customer data)
- ltv_to_cac
- payback_months (CAC / (ARPU × gross_margin%))
- burn_multiple_q1_2026 (sum of Jan–Mar net burn divided by net new ARR from Dec-2025 end to Mar-2026 end)
- rule_of_40_monthly ((MoM growth rate in % + profit margin % for 2026-03); provide a single numeric value)
- magic_number_q1_2026 (Q1 2026 net new ARR divided by Q4 2025 S&M spend)
- runway_months (cash_balance in 2026-03 / net burn in 2026-03)

2) Create output/investor_update.yaml using a standard monthly update structure with:
- metrics.arr.current, metrics.arr.prior_month, metrics.arr.delta
- metrics.mrr.current and metrics.mrr.growth_mom
- metrics.customers.total, metrics.customers.new, metrics.customers.churned
- metrics.ndr, metrics.burn_rate (2026-03 net burn), metrics.runway_months, metrics.cash_balance
- highlights (2–3 bullets), wins (3–5 bullets), challenges (2–3 bullets), next_month_priorities (3–5 bullets), asks (at least 2 asks)
Ensure numeric values are consistent with output/metrics_summary.json.

3) Build a cohort retention table at output/cohort_analysis.csv. Use input/cohorts.csv and output a matrix with columns: cohort,m0_retention,m1_retention,m3_retention,m6_retention where each retention column is (MRR_at_that_mark / MRR_month0) rounded to 2 decimals when data is available. Include at least the cohorts present in the input.

4) Write a diagnostic using the PULSE framework at output/pulse_diagnostic.md focused on the churn spike and sub-100% revenue retention. Include the five sections with clear headings: Pattern, Upstream, Leverage, So-What, Experiment. Reference the specific months (notably 2026-02 and 2026-03), NDR/GDR values, and propose at least one testable experiment with success and kill criteria.

Inputs to use:
- input/mrr_components.csv — monthly MRR waterfall components (starting_mrr,new_mrr,expansion_mrr,reactivation_mrr,contraction_mrr,churned_mrr,ending_mrr)
- input/customers.csv — customer counts and unit economics by month (customers_start,new_customers,reactivated_customers,churned_customers,customers_end,avg_arpu,gross_margin_pct)
- input/sales_marketing.csv — monthly Sales & Marketing spend
- input/cash.csv — monthly total_expenses, revenue_recognized, cash_balance (for runway and burn)
- input/cohorts.csv — cohort MRR snapshots (m0,m1,m3,m6) for recent cohorts
- input/model.yaml — model/stage context

Write only to the output/ paths specified above. Use the standard SaaS metric formulas and keep calculations internally consistent across files.
